# Review: `deepmind/905.lean` — Erdős Problem 905

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 905`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to the source.
- [ ] **Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem.**
  - **Issue:** The module docstring contains only the heading and reference link. A brief plain-English summary of the problem (e.g., the Bollobás-Erdős conjecture statement) should be added here. The description currently lives only in the theorem docstring.
- N/A Optional bibliography entries.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos905 ... end Erdos905`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos905`).
- [x] `open SimpleGraph Finset` appears **before** the namespace block.

## 5. Custom Definitions

- N/A — No `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations in this file.

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_905` (snake_case, matching the namespace number).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib `snake_case` convention.
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same** attribute line: `@[category research open, AMS 5]`.
- [x] Main theorem has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [ ] **Theorem docstring should ideally reference source paper(s).**
  - **Minor:** The docstring mentions "Bollobás-Erdős conjecture" but does not include a citation key (e.g., `[BolEr76]`). This is a soft recommendation ("ideally").

### `answer()` usage

The problem is phrased as a direct assertion: "Every graph with $n$ vertices and more than $n^2/4$ edges contains an edge which is in at least $n/6$ triangles." This is **Case 3** (direct assertion).

- [x] The theorem type is `P` directly, with no `answer()` wrapper — correct for Case 3.
- [x] Universal quantification over `n` and `G` is via theorem parameters, which is appropriate since there is no `answer()`.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute: `category research open`.
- [x] Value is a valid category (`research open`).
- [x] `research open` theorem contains `sorry` in its proof body.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute: `AMS 5`.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number is listed (5 — Combinatorics).
- [x] Numbers are in ascending order (single number).
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] `5` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses the multi-line `by` / `sorry` form (`:= by\n  sorry`), which is acceptable.
- [x] No `sorry` in any `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` (none present).
- [x] No informal proof sketches for this open research problem.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A — No variants declared.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable (`∃`, `∧`, `∩`, `≥`, `ℕ`, `ℝ`).
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` — not verified in this review.
- [ ] All five custom linters pass — not verified in this review.

---

## Summary

**Overall: PASS with one required fix.**

| # | Item | Status |
|---|------|--------|
| 1 | Copyright Header | ✅ Pass |
| 2 | Import Statement | ✅ Pass |
| 3 | Module Docstring | ⚠️ Missing plain-English description |
| 4 | Namespace | ✅ Pass |
| 5 | Custom Definitions | N/A |
| 6 | Theorem Statements | ✅ Pass (minor: no citation key in docstring) |
| 7 | Category Attribute | ✅ Pass |
| 8 | AMS Attribute | ✅ Pass |
| 9 | Proof Bodies | ✅ Pass |
| 10 | Variants | N/A |
| 11 | Code Quality | ✅ Pass |
| 12 | Build Verification | ⬜ Not tested |

### Required fix

1. **§3 Module Docstring — add plain-English description.** The `/-! ... -/` block needs a brief summary of the Bollobás-Erdős conjecture (e.g., "Every graph on $n$ vertices with more than $n^2/4$ edges contains an edge in at least $n/6$ triangles.").

### Recommended (non-blocking)

2. **§6 Theorem Docstring — add citation key.** Consider adding a bibliography entry and referencing it (e.g., `[BolEr76]`) in the theorem docstring.

### Formalization correctness note

The formalization correctly captures the problem: "more than $n^2/4$ edges" maps to the strict inequality on `edgeFinset.card`, and "at least $n/6$ triangles containing an edge $\{u,v\}$" is encoded via the cardinality of the common-neighbor set `neighborFinset u ∩ neighborFinset v`. The `[DecidableRel G.Adj]` instance is appropriate for `Finset`-based graph operations.

file.content:
/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erdős Problem 905

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/905](https://www.erdosproblems.com/905)
-/

open SimpleGraph Finset

namespace Erdos905

/--
Erdős Problem 905 (Bollobás-Erdős conjecture):
Every graph with $n$ vertices and more than $n^2/4$ edges contains an edge
which is in at least $n/6$ triangles.

A triangle containing edge $\{u, v\}$ corresponds to a common neighbor of $u$ and $v$,
i.e., a vertex $w$ adjacent to both $u$ and $v$.
-/
@[category research open, AMS 5]
theorem erdos_905 (n : ℕ) (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) [DecidableRel G.Adj] :
    (G.edgeFinset.card : ℝ) > (n : ℝ) ^ 2 / 4 →
    ∃ u v : Fin n, G.Adj u v ∧
      ((G.neighborFinset u ∩ G.neighborFinset v).card : ℝ) ≥ (n : ℝ) / 6 := by
  sorry

end Erdos905

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erdős Problem 905

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/905](https://www.erdosproblems.com/905)
-/

open SimpleGraph Finset

namespace Erdos905

/--
Erdős Problem 905 (Bollobás-Erdős conjecture):
Every graph with $n$ vertices and more than $n^2/4$ edges contains an edge
which is in at least $n/6$ triangles.

A triangle containing edge $\{u, v\}$ corresponds to a common neighbor of $u$ and $v$,
i.e., a vertex $w$ adjacent to both $u$ and $v$.
-/
@[category research open, AMS 5]
theorem erdos_905 (n : ℕ) (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) [DecidableRel G.Adj] :
    (G.edgeFinset.card : ℝ) > (n : ℝ) ^ 2 / 4 →
    ∃ u v : Fin n, G.Adj u v ∧
      ((G.neighborFinset u ∩ G.neighborFinset v).card : ℝ) ≥ (n : ℝ) / 6 := by
  sorry

end Erdos905
